generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor per axis, providing high-resolution position feedback for precise control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionDataStream
]

instance GearboxPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary encoder positioned behind the gearbox per axis, ensuring redundant position measurement for path repeatability and fault detection."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxPositionDataStream
]

instance CurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring input current per axis, critical for monitoring motor performance and detecting anomalies."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CurrentDataStream
]

instance TorqueSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring torque output per axis, enabling dynamic behavior analysis and joint friction identification."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TorqueDataStream
]

instance AngularVelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring angular velocity per axis, supporting real-time kinematic analysis and control validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AngularVelocityDataStream
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorPositionDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
    base:desc "Time-series position data from motor encoder transmitted via MQTT protocol for real-time monitoring."
]

instance GearboxPositionDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxPositionEncoder
    base:desc "Time-series position data from gearbox encoder transmitted via MQTT protocol for redundant validation."
]

instance CurrentDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
    base:desc "Time-series current data from physical axis transmitted via MQTT protocol for dynamic analysis."
]

instance TorqueDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TorqueSensor
    base:desc "Time-series torque data from physical axis transmitted via MQTT protocol for friction and load analysis."
]

instance AngularVelocityDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AngularVelocitySensor
    base:desc "Time-series angular velocity data from physical axis transmitted via MQTT protocol for kinematic validation."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance RobotKinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML-based kinematic model of the MABI MAX 100 serial robot with six axes, defining geometric relationships and joint configurations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler, SimulinkEnabler
]

instance RobotDynamicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML and Simulink-based dynamic model capturing motor-gearbox interactions, load effects, and control behavior."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler, SimulinkEnabler
]

instance JointFrictionAnalysisModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Specialized model for identifying and quantifying joint friction using aggregated sensor data traces."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler, MontiArcEnabler
]

instance PhysicalAxisData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated and abstracted contextual data traces from all physical axes (position, torque, current, velocity) preprocessed for modeling."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionDataStream, GearboxPositionDataStream, CurrentDataStream, TorqueDataStream, AngularVelocityDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler, SimulinkEnabler, AASEnabler
]

instance ControlLogicData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured data representing Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller inputs/outputs (position, speed, acceleration commands)."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionDataStream, AngularVelocityDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler, AASEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML v1.7 implementation for system architecture modeling, including component-connector descriptions and state machine behavioral modeling."
    DTDFVocab:enables RobotArchitectureService, FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance SimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink for detailed behavioral simulation and dynamic system analysis across multiple abstraction layers."
    DTDFVocab:enables DynamicSimulationService, RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance AASEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell (AAS) for standardized data exchange and semantic interoperability between physical and digital domains."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataIntegrationService
]

instance MQTTEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation for lightweight, publish-subscribe communication of sensor time-series data from physical to virtual."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTransmissionService
]

instance MontiArcEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc architecture description language for defining components, interfaces, and composition in functional/logical architectures."
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralModelingService
]

// C6: Services
instance RobotArchitectureService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing SysML-based structural and behavioral representations of the MABI MAX 100 robot across requirements, functional, and physical layers."
    DTDFVocab:provides RobotStructureInsight, ComponentInteractionInsight
]

instance DynamicSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling Simulink-based dynamic simulations of robot behavior under various operational conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides DynamicBehaviorInsight, ControlValidationInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service identifying joint friction patterns using aggregated sensor data and specialized models."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionIdentificationInsight, WearPredictionInsight
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing dashboard-based visualization of real-time sensor data against simulated models."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalStateInsight, AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance DataIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service ensuring standardized data exchange between heterogeneous modeling tools via Asset Administration Shell."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataConsistencyInsight, CrossLayerTraceabilityInsight
]

instance DataTransmissionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing MQTT-based transmission of sensor time-series data from physical robot to digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataStreamInsight
]

instance BehavioralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service defining and validating behavioral models using MontiArc automata and SysML state machines."
    DTDFVocab:provides BehavioralConsistencyInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RobotStructureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural overview of the MABI MAX 100 robot including component hierarchy, interfaces, and connector relationships as modeled in SysML."
]

instance ComponentInteractionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detailed interaction patterns between motors, gearboxes, and controller components across functional and physical layers."
]

instance DynamicBehaviorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulated dynamic behavior of the robot under various load conditions, validated against real-time sensor data."
]

instance ControlValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comparison between commanded control inputs (from SINUMERIK) and actual physical responses for validation purposes."
]

instance FrictionIdentificationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Automated identification of joint friction characteristics using aggregated torque and position data traces."
]

instance WearPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analysis of component wear based on friction patterns and operational history."
]

instance OperationalStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time dashboard visualization comparing measured physical states against simulated digital twin predictions."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Automated detection of operational anomalies through deviation analysis between physical measurements and digital models."
]

instance DataConsistencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Verification of data consistency across heterogeneous modeling tools via Asset Administration Shell integration."
]

instance CrossLayerTraceabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Traceability links between physical sensor data, logical models, and functional requirements across all abstraction layers."
]

instance DataStreamInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Monitoring of MQTT-based data transmission quality and latency between physical sensors and digital twin."
]

instance BehavioralConsistencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation of behavioral models against real-time system responses using MontiArc automata and SysML state machines."
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "PMSM actuator per robot axis with maximum speed of 75 deg/s, controlled via Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller."
]

instance GearboxSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Reduction gearbox system per axis connecting motor to robot joints, ensuring torque amplification and motion transmission."
]

instance SINUMERIK840DslController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller governing robot actuation with position, speed, and acceleration input capabilities."
]

// C1: System Under Study
instance MABIMax100Robot : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Six-axis serial robot (MABI MAX 100) with modular hierarchical architecture operating in industrial automation domain."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingAutomationEnvironment
]

instance ManufacturingAutomationEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Industrial automation setting with safety cells enforcing operational boundaries for the MABI MAX 100 robot."
    base:contains MABIMax100Robot
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin (DT) constellation** in this context is implicitly structured around **MBSE methodologies** and **architecture description languages (ADLs)** to orchestrate system-wide coordination. Key aspects include:
  1. **Architecture Patterns**: Systems are modeled using **component & connector perspectives** (e.g., **SysML IBDs, ACME, AADL, MontiArc**), defining **interfaces, interconnections, and composition** of DT components.
  2. **Resource Management**: Heterogeneous stakeholders and perspectives (e.g., requirements, functional, logical, physical layers) are integrated via **multi-notation modeling tools** (e.g., **SysML, Simulink, C++/Java code**), requiring **cross-tool synchronization** for cohesive DT operation.
  3. **System-Wide Coordination**: Behavior is described using **state machines (SysML v1.7), MontiArc Automaton, or simulation tools**, enabling **dynamic coordination** between physical and digital layers. However, no explicit orchestration framework (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures) is mentioned."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin lifecycle stages are structured into **multi-level abstraction phases** aligned with Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) methodologies. These phases include:
  1. **Requirements Phase**: Defines functional and non-functional requirements for the Digital Twin, serving as the foundational input for subsequent modeling.
  2. **Functional Architecture Phase**: Utilizes **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)** to decompose the system into **components, connectors, interfaces, and compositions**, abstracting high-level functional behavior.
  3. **Logical Architecture Phase**: Refines the functional model into a **logical architecture** using ADLs (e.g., **MontiArc Automaton** or **SysML state machines**) to define **behavioral dynamics**, including state transitions and interactions between components.
  4. **Product/Technical Layer Phase**: Implements the logical architecture into a **physical/technical representation** via tools like **MathWorks Simulink, simulation environments (e.g., [29], [30]), or general-purpose programming languages (e.g., C++, Java)** to ensure operational fidelity.
  Transition management between phases is governed by **iterative refinement** and **model integration**, where heterogeneous models (e.g., SysML for architecture, Simulink for behavior) are harmonized. Lifecycle governance is implicit in MBSE, emphasizing **stakeholder alignment** and **consistency validation** across abstraction levels to mitigate complexity in large-scale systems (e.g., manufacturing or robotics)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin’s **model fidelity** is ensured through **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., SysML, ADLs) and integration with simulation tools (Simulink), but explicit **validation and verification (V&V) techniques** or **uncertainty quantification** are not detailed in the provided documents. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** highlights the need for **active software components** to interpret and process **one-way automated data flows** from physical objects, mitigating passive data collection limitations. **Quality assurance** relies on the integration of **heterogeneous tools and stakeholder perspectives** [31], though no specific error detection/correction mechanisms or formal validation protocols (e.g., model calibration, traceability matrices) are described."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Shadow system leverages **MQTT protocol** for **lightweight, publish-subscribe-based communication** between physical sensors and the digital representation, ensuring efficient data streaming. While the document does not specify network topology, bandwidth, or latency requirements, the use of MQTT implies support for **low-power, high-frequency sensor data transmission** in industrial environments. No explicit details on reliability, redundancy, or security protocols (e.g., TLS, encryption) are provided, though the integration with modeling tools (e.g., Simulink, SysML) suggests adherence to standard industrial communication practices. The system’s passive nature precludes active control flows, limiting technical connection details to data-centric protocols."
]
